title: A bill to amend the Public Health Service Act to reauthorize the Stop, Observe, Ask, and Respond to Health and Wellness Training Program.
plain_summary: This bill keeps a health training program called SOAR running for five more years until 2029. The program was supposed to end in 2024, but now it will get more money to continue. The bill also takes back twenty million dollars that the government had set aside but never used. This money comes from the Department of Health and Human Services.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
subjects: Social work, volunteer service, charitable organizations

sections.title: Section 1  Reauthorization of the SOAR to Health and Wellness Training Program
sections.text: This section makes two changes to an existing law. First, it extends funding for the Stop, Observe, Ask, and Respond to Health and Wellness Training Program. The program was previously funded through the year two thousand twenty four. Now it will be funded through the year two thousand twenty nine. Second, it takes back twenty million dollars from unused money that the Department of Health and Human Services has set aside for special expenses. This money will no longer be available for the department to spend.
